input.prompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

data.content: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-09T00:56:25.462Z</current_datetime>

Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.reasoningText: The user wants to know what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. I can use `dotnet new blazor --dry-run` to show what files would be created.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.content:
Here's what `dotnet new blazor --interactivity Server` would generate in `./test-output`:

**App core (6 files):**
- `Program.cs`, `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json`
- `Properties/launchSettings.json`
- `Components/App.razor`, `Components/Routes.razor`, `Components/_Imports.razor`

**Layout (7 files):**
- `MainLayout.razor` + `.css`
- `NavMenu.razor` + `.css` + `.js`
- `ReconnectModal.razor` + `.css` + `.js` — specific to Server interactivity (handles SignalR reconnection)

**Pages (5 files):**
- `Home.razor`, `Counter.razor`, `Weather.razor`, `Error.razor`, `NotFound.razor`

**Static assets (2 + Bootstrap):**
- `wwwroot/app.css`, `wwwroot/favicon.png`
- Full Bootstrap CSS & JS distribution (~48 files with source maps)

**Total: ~68 files.** A post-creation `dotnet restore` would also run automatically.

Ready to create it when you say the word.
